examples: Mrs. Thatcher was almost crazed; and Aunt Polly, also.
Here, a neighbor father, crazed with grief at the death of his son, killed himself, and the moon implies in its gaze that the poet should do so, too.
He gets into the game by jumping a fear-crazed soldier and stealing his suit.
How can a retail store possibly meet the expectations of these crazed fans?
